source: macro_df (glmacro_master_alldata.parquet, Growth Lab) for indicators with broad coverage; Hanson & Sigman (2021) replication archive (Harvard Dataverse DOI 10.7910/DVN/IFZXQX) for the five series not in macro_df.
description: Capacity-adjacent indicators that did NOT enter the Hanson & Sigman latent factor. Most appeared in their Table 3 as convergent-validity checks; the rest are governance / quality-of-government indices with good coverage. The version with the broadest temporal coverage was selected for each indicator.

indicators.label: Government effectiveness
indicators.description: Captures perceptions of public-service quality, civil-service independence from political pressure, and the quality and credibility of policy formulation and implementation. Standard-normal score, approximately -2.5 to +2.5.

indicators.label: Rule of law
indicators.description: Captures perceptions of contract enforcement, property rights, policing, and the courts, plus the likelihood of crime and violence. Standard-normal score, approximately -2.5 to +2.5.

indicators.label: Regulatory quality
indicators.description: Captures perceptions of the government's ability to formulate and implement sound regulations that promote private-sector development. Standard-normal score, approximately -2.5 to +2.5.

indicators.label: Control of corruption
indicators.description: Captures perceptions of the extent to which public power is exercised for private gain — petty and grand corruption plus state capture by elites. Standard-normal score, approximately -2.5 to +2.5.

indicators.label: Voice and accountability
indicators.description: Captures perceptions of citizens' ability to participate in selecting their government, plus freedoms of expression, association, and media. Standard-normal score, approximately -2.5 to +2.5.

indicators.label: Political stability and absence of violence
indicators.dict_label: Political Stability and Absence of Violence/Terrorism, Estimate
indicators.description: Captures perceptions of the likelihood that the government will be destabilised or overthrown by unconstitutional or violent means, including politically-motivated violence and terrorism. Standard-normal score, approximately -2.5 to +2.5.

indicators.label: Public sector management & institutions (cluster avg)
indicators.dict_label: CPIA public sector management and institutions cluster average (1=low to 6=high)
indicators.description: Cluster average of the five CPIA public-sector criteria: property rights and rule-based governance; budgetary and financial management; revenue mobilisation efficiency; quality of public administration; transparency, accountability and corruption. IDA-eligible (low-income) countries only.

indicators.label: IDA Resource Allocation Index (CPIA overall)
indicators.description: The overall CPIA composite: average of 16 criteria across economic management, structural policies, social inclusion/equity, and public-sector management. IDA uses it to allocate concessional lending. IDA-eligible countries only.

indicators.label: Stateness
indicators.description: BTI composite of monopoly on use of force, state identity, freedom from religious-dogma interference, and basic administration. 1 (low) to 10 (high). Developing and transitioning countries only.

indicators.label: Rule of law
indicators.description: BTI sub-index of separation of powers, judicial independence, prosecution of office abuse, and civil rights. 1 (low) to 10 (high).

indicators.label: Governance performance (management index)
indicators.description: BTI Management Index: steering capability, resource efficiency, consensus-building, and international cooperation. 1 (low) to 10 (high).

indicators.label: Basic administration
indicators.description: BTI sub-component of stateness — whether functioning administrative structures exist throughout the country to provide basic public services. 1 (low) to 10 (high).

indicators.label: Monopoly on use of force
indicators.dict_label: Monopoly on the Use of Force
indicators.description: BTI sub-component of stateness — degree to which the state's monopoly on legitimate violence is established nation-wide. 1 (low) to 10 (high).

indicators.label: Corruption Perceptions Index
indicators.description: Composite of expert and business surveys on perceived public-sector corruption. Pre-2012 the scale was 0–10; from 2012 onwards 0–100 (higher = cleaner). The 2011/2012 jump is an instrument change, not a behavioural change.

indicators.label: ICRG quality of government composite
indicators.source: PRS Group ICRG via QoG Institute
indicators.dict_label: ICRG Indicator of Quality of Government
indicators.description: Mean of three PRS International Country Risk Guide sub-indicators — corruption, law and order, and bureaucratic quality — rescaled 0–1 (higher = better). Deepest panel of any QoG composite: 1984 onwards, >5,000 country-years.

indicators.label: Impartial public administration (Rothstein & Teorell)
indicators.source: QoG Institute expert survey, Rothstein & Teorell (2008)
indicators.description: Expert assessment of whether public officials apply the same procedures to similar cases regardless of who is involved. Cross-sectional QoG survey, ~50 countries, single round c. 2008.

indicators.label: Rational-legal state capacity (Hendrix 2010)
indicators.description: Factor-analytic index combining bureaucratic quality and tax-composition measures to capture the rational-legal (Weberian) dimension of state capacity.

indicators.label: Myers age-heaping index (log)
indicators.source: Lee & Zhang (2017), via H&S replication archive
indicators.description: Log of the Myers blended index of age heaping in national censuses. High values = citizens reporting ages ending in 0 or 5 = the state has little reason to know exact ages = LESS information capacity. Inverse signal.

indicators.label: Public services indicator (FSI)
indicators.source: Fund for Peace Fragile States Index, via H&S replication
indicators.description: FSI sub-component rating the state's failure to deliver basic public goods — water, sanitation, electricity, transport, education, health. Higher = worse delivery. Inverse signal.

indicators.label: Fragile States Index
indicators.source: Fund for Peace, via H&S replication
indicators.description: Aggregate fragility score across 12 social, economic, and political indicators (range ~18–115). Higher = more fragile. Inverse signal.
